codeSnippet:
interface ConceptRepositoryPort {
    fun save(concept: Concept): Concept
    fun findById(id: Long): Concept?
    fun findByConceptId(conceptId: String): Concept?
    fun findAll(pageable: Pageable): Page<Concept>
    fun findByCategory(category: ConceptCategory, pageable: Pageable): Page<Concept>
    fun findByLevel(level: ConceptLevel, pageable: Pageable): Page<Concept>
    fun findAllWithSynonyms(): List<Concept>
    fun delete(id: Long)
    fun existsByConceptId(conceptId: String): Boolean
}
description: Application layer defines a port interface for repository — the dependency points inward, decoupling domain/application from infrastructure per Clean Architecture rules

codeSnippet:
interface ConceptIndexingPort {
    fun indexConceptIndex(concept: Concept, conceptIndex: ConceptIndex)
    fun bulkIndex(entries: List<Pair<Concept, ConceptIndex>>)
    fun deleteByConceptId(conceptId: String)
    fun createOrUpdateIndex()
    fun updateSynonyms(synonymMap: Map<String, List<String>>)
}
description: An outbound port for search indexing — the application layer defines the interface and infrastructure implements it, following hexagonal architecture's port-adapter boundary
ConceptIndexingAdapter implements ConceptIndexingPort, making it a classic adapter that bridges the application port to the concrete OpenSearch infrastructure
ConceptRepositoryAdapter wraps a Spring Data JPA repository and adapts it to the domain-facing ConceptRepositoryPort interface, converting between JPA entities and domain models
Concept is a rich domain aggregate with private constructor, factory methods (create/restore), and invariant guards — a textbook DDD aggregate root pattern
CodeLocation is an immutable data class with validation invariants in init block — a canonical value object representing a code location without its own identity

codeSnippet:
data class CreateConceptCommand(
    val conceptId: String,
    val name: String,
    val category: ConceptCategory,
    val level: ConceptLevel,
    val description: String,
    val synonyms: List<String> = emptyList()
)

data class UpdateConceptCommand(
    val name: String? = null,
    val category: ConceptCategory? = null,
    val level: ConceptLevel? = null,
    val description: String? = null,
    val synonyms: List<String>? = null
)
description: Command objects (CreateConceptCommand, UpdateConceptCommand, ConceptResultDto) use Kotlin data class for automatic equals/hashCode/copy/toString — idiomatic DTO pattern throughout the application layer
companion object holds factory methods (create/restore) on the Concept and ConceptIndex domain classes — Kotlin's idiomatic equivalent of static factory methods
ConceptJpaEntity.fromDomain() and toDomain() are static factory methods that encapsulate creation and mapping logic — the Factory pattern applied for domain-to-entity translation
@Bean in a @Configuration class produces a singleton-scoped OpenSearchClient — Spring manages a single shared instance for the application lifetime
ConceptCategory and ConceptLevel are Kotlin enums that constrain valid values for concept classification — type-safe enumeration used throughout domain and application layers
Domain-specific exceptions extend BusinessException with typed ErrorCodes — structured exception hierarchy that allows the presentation layer to return consistent error responses
ConceptSearchPort, ConceptIndexingPort, and ConceptRepositoryPort are Kotlin interfaces that define the contracts between layers — enabling dependency inversion and testability
Spring MVC annotations (@RestController, @RequestMapping, @GetMapping, @RequestParam, @PathVariable) are used pervasively to configure HTTP routing and parameter binding declaratively
ApiResponse<Page<ConceptResultDto>> and ResponseEntity<T> show nested generic type parameters used to wrap typed responses — generics enforce type safety across the response chain
GraphService uses higher-order lambdas (map, flatMap, filter, groupBy, distinctBy) extensively to transform domain collections into graph data — idiomatic Kotlin functional style

codeSnippet:
fun toDomain(): Concept = Concept.restore(
    id = id,
    conceptId = conceptId,
    name = name,
    category = ConceptCategory.valueOf(category),
    level = ConceptLevel.valueOf(level),
    description = description ?: "",
    synonyms = synonyms.map { it.synonym },
    relatedConceptIds = relations.map { it.targetConcept.conceptId }
)
description: toDomain() and fromDomain() methods on JPA entities add domain-conversion behavior directly to the class — the idiomatic Kotlin pattern of attaching behavior to types for clean layer mapping
ConceptJpaEntity uses JPA/Hibernate ORM annotations (@Entity, @Table, @OneToMany, @ManyToOne, CascadeType.ALL) to map the Concept aggregate to relational tables with relationship management
RoutingDataSource routes queries to MASTER or REPLICA based on transaction read-only flag — implements read/write splitting to leverage MySQL replication for scalable reads
LazyConnectionDataSourceProxy defers actual JDBC connection acquisition until the first statement executes — an optimization that reduces connection pool pressure for read-only transactions
CorsConfig explicitly permits cross-origin requests from the local frontend dev server ports — necessary because the frontend (Vite) runs on a different origin than the Spring Boot API
ConceptController exposes a full CRUD REST API using standard HTTP verbs (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE) with resource-based URL paths — textbook RESTful resource design
bulkIndex() batches multiple documents into a single OpenSearch Bulk API call, avoiding N individual index requests for high-throughput sync operations
The OpenSearch index mapping with custom analyzers (Nori Korean, edge-ngram) builds an inverted index over concept names, synonyms, descriptions, and code snippets for full-text search
Concept has a private constructor forcing all instantiation through factory methods — encapsulating invariant validation so the object can never be in an invalid state
SearchService depends only on the ConceptSearchPort abstraction, with no knowledge of OpenSearch — the infrastructure detail is hidden behind an interface abstraction

ConceptRepositoryAdapter, ConceptIndexRepositoryAdapter, ConceptIndexingAdapter, and ConceptSearchAdapter all implement shared port interfaces — callers work with the interface type and the correct implementation is injected at runtime
code-dictionary is an independent Spring Boot service with its own DB and domain — a standalone microservice deployed separately from other services in the MSA platform
